description: Colombian drug cartel and paramilitary group formed around 2004 by Wilber Varela and Diego Rastrojo. It funds itself through cocaine, marijuana, heroin trafficking, and illegal gold mining. At its height, it was one of the most important drug trafficking organizations in Colombia, operating mainly in Valle del Cauca and expanding to other parts of Colombia and western Venezuela. The group is considered an heir of the Cali cartel.

country_links.evidence_quote: In May 2012 one of the leaders of Los Rastrojos, Javier Antonio Calle Serna (alias "Comba"), handed himself in to US authorities in Aruba.
country_links.context: One of the leaders of Los Rastrojos surrendered to US authorities in Aruba.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: Colombia , Venezuela , Ecuador , Central America , Mexico
country_links.context: The organization has territory in Ecuador.

country_links.evidence_quote: shipping it themselves through Central America and Mexico... The Rastrojos move drugs primarily up the Pacific Coast to Central America and Mexico where they sell it to Mexican drug traffickers...
country_links.context: The group ships drugs through Central America and Mexico, where they sell to Mexican traffickers.

country_links.evidence_quote: Mexican drug traffickers who take it to the United States... northwards into the US on aircraft and go-fast boats.
country_links.context: The group's drugs are ultimately taken to the United States via Mexican traffickers and also shipped northwards into the US.

country_links.evidence_quote: The group became independent after the murder of its main founder in Venezuela in 2008... They also have control of one of the primary smuggling routes into Venezuela...
country_links.context: The group became independent after the murder of its main founder in Venezuela in 2008, and they have control of smuggling routes into Venezuela, with members killed or arrested there.
